title: Set up Convex real-time backend in a Next.js App Router project
summary: Full Convex integration for Next.js â€” install the SDK, bootstrap the project, write typed server functions (queries, mutations, actions), configure the React provider, and connect components with reactive hooks.
declaration: I will set up Convex as the real-time backend for this Next.js project. Convex replaces a traditional database + ORM + API layer with a single TypeScript-first platform. It provides reactive queries (components auto-update when data changes), server-side mutations and actions, file storage, and scheduled jobs. If you only need a traditional REST API or already have Prisma/Drizzle configured, tell me before I begin â€” Convex works differently.
instructions:
## Set up Convex in Next.js (App Router) ### 0. Prerequisites - Node.js >= 18. - A Convex account at https://dashboard.convex.dev (sign in with GitHub or Google â€” free tier is generous). - The project uses Next.js App Router (`app/` directory). ### 1. Install dependencies ```bash npm install convex ``` - `convex` is the only package needed for both server functions and client-side hooks. - Do NOT install `@convex-dev/auth` unless you want Convex-native auth â€” standard Clerk/Auth.js integrations use a different approach. ### 2. Initialize Convex ```bash npx convex dev ``` - This command authenticates your CLI, prompts you to create or select a Convex project, and generates: - `convex.json` â€” project configuration (commit this). - `convex/_generated/` â€” auto-generated API types (do NOT edit; commit the directory but add to `.gitignore` if you prefer). - Leave this terminal running during development â€” it watches the `convex/` directory and hot-reloads functions. - The command also prints your deployment URL: `https://.convex.cloud`. This becomes `NEXT_PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL`. ### 3. Environment variables - Create or update `.env.local`: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL=https://.convex.cloud ``` - The `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix is required â€” the client SDK needs this in the browser. - Do NOT add a secret key to `.env.local`. Convex security is managed through function-level auth checks, not API keys. - Verify `.env.local` is in `.gitignore`. ### 4. Wrap the app with ConvexClientProvider - Create `app/providers.tsx`: ```tsx 'use client' import { ConvexProvider, ConvexReactClient } from 'convex/react' const convex = new ConvexReactClient(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL!) export function Providers({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return {children} } ``` - Update `app/layout.tsx` to wrap children: ```tsx import { Providers } from './providers' export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return ( {children} ) } ``` - The `ConvexReactClient` instance is created once outside the component â€” do NOT create it inside a component render or useEffect. ### 5. Define your schema (`convex/schema.ts`) - Create the `convex/` directory in your project root: ```typescript import { defineSchema, defineTable } from 'convex/server' import { v } from 'convex/values' export default defineSchema({ tasks: defineTable({ text: v.string(), isCompleted: v.boolean(), userId: v.optional(v.string()), createdAt: v.number(), }), }) ``` - The schema is optional but strongly recommended â€” it enables TypeScript inference and runtime validation. - Convex validators (`v.string()`, `v.number()`, `v.boolean()`, `v.array()`, `v.object()`) mirror TypeScript types. - After saving, `npx convex dev` automatically pushes the schema to the cloud. ### 6. Write server functions #### 6a. Query (read data â€” reactive) Create `convex/tasks.ts`: ```typescript import { query, mutation } from './_generated/server' import { v } from 'convex/values' export const list = query({ args: {}, handler: async (ctx) => { return await ctx.db.query('tasks').order('desc').collect() }, }) export const listByUser = query({ args: { userId: v.string() }, handler: async (ctx, args) => { return await ctx.db .query('tasks') .filter((q) => q.eq(q.field('userId'), args.userId)) .collect() }, }) ``` - Queries are read-only and run reactively â€” any client subscribed to a query re-renders automatically when the underlying data changes. - Do NOT perform side effects (HTTP calls, writes) inside a query. Use mutations or actions for that. #### 6b. Mutation (write data) ```typescript export const create = mutation({ args: { text: v.string(), userId: v.optional(v.string()), }, handler: async (ctx, args) => { const taskId = await ctx.db.insert('tasks', { text: args.text, isCompleted: false, userId: args.userId, createdAt: Date.now(), }) return taskId }, }) export const toggleComplete = mutation({ args: { id: v.id('tasks') }, handler: async (ctx, args) => { const task = await ctx.db.get(args.id) if (!task) throw new Error('Task not found') await ctx.db.patch(args.id, { isCompleted: !task.isCompleted }) }, }) export const remove = mutation({ args: { id: v.id('tasks') }, handler: async (ctx, args) => { await ctx.db.delete(args.id) }, }) ``` - `v.id('tableName')` is a typed document ID â€” Convex validates it at runtime. - `ctx.db.patch` does a partial update; `ctx.db.replace` replaces the entire document. #### 6c. Action (external API calls, long-running work) ```typescript import { action } from './_generated/server' import { v } from 'convex/values' export const generateSummary = action({ args: { taskId: v.id('tasks') }, handler: async (ctx, args) => { // Actions can call external APIs const task = await ctx.runQuery(api.tasks.getById, { id: args.taskId }) const response = await fetch('https://api.openai.com/v1/...', { ... }) const summary = await response.json() await ctx.runMutation(api.tasks.updateSummary, { id: args.taskId, summary }) return summary }, }) ``` - Actions can call external HTTP APIs and run database operations via `ctx.runQuery` / `ctx.runMutation`. - Actions do NOT run reactively â€” they are called imperatively (like an RPC). ### 7. Use hooks in React components ```tsx 'use client' import { useQuery, useMutation } from 'convex/react' import { api } from '@/convex/_generated/api' export function TaskList() { const tasks = useQuery(api.tasks.list) const createTask = useMutation(api.tasks.create) const toggleTask = useMutation(api.tasks.toggleComplete) if (tasks === undefined) return
Loading...
return (
{tasks.map((task) => (
toggleTask({ id: task._id })}> {task.isCompleted ? 'âś“' : 'â—‹'} {task.text}
))}
) } ``` - `useQuery` returns `undefined` while loading, then the data â€” always check for `undefined`. - `useMutation` returns a function that sends the mutation to Convex. It handles optimistic updates automatically. - `api` is the auto-generated typed API object from `convex/_generated/api.ts`. Import from there â€” never hardcode function names as strings. ### 8. Authentication integration - Convex integrates with Clerk, Auth.js, and other providers. #### With Clerk: ```bash npm install @clerk/nextjs ``` Update `app/providers.tsx`: ```tsx 'use client' import { ClerkProvider, useAuth } from '@clerk/nextjs' import { ConvexProviderWithClerk } from 'convex/react-clerk' import { ConvexReactClient } from 'convex/react' const convex = new ConvexReactClient(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL!) export function Providers({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return ( {children} ) } ``` In server functions, access the user identity: ```typescript export const listMyTasks = query({ handler: async (ctx) => { const identity = await ctx.auth.getUserIdentity() if (!identity) throw new Error('Unauthenticated') return ctx.db .query('tasks') .filter((q) => q.eq(q.field('userId'), identity.subject)) .collect() }, }) ``` ### 9. Deployment (production) ```bash npx convex deploy ``` - This pushes your functions to the production Convex deployment. - Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL` in your Vercel/hosting platform to the production URL (found in Convex dashboard). - You do NOT need to run `convex dev` in production â€” it's development-only. ### Common errors & fixes - **`ConvexProvider not found` / hooks throw outside provider** â†’ The component using `useQuery`/`useMutation` is not wrapped in `ConvexProvider`. Check that `Providers` wraps the root layout and that `'use client'` is set on the providers file. - **`useQuery` always returns `undefined`** â†’ The query function doesn't exist or has a typo. Check that the function is exported from its `convex/*.ts` file and that `npx convex dev` is running. - **`Argument validation error`** â†’ You passed wrong types to a mutation/query. The validator in the `args` block is enforced at runtime. Check the function signature. - **Changes not appearing** â†’ `npx convex dev` is not running. It must be active in development to push function changes. - **`Cannot use import/require in convex functions`** â†’ Convex functions run in a sandboxed environment. You cannot use native Node.js modules (`fs`, `crypto`, etc.) in queries/mutations. Use actions for external calls. - **Data not reactive / component not re-rendering** â†’ You are calling the function directly instead of using `useQuery`. Only `useQuery` creates a reactive subscription. - **TypeScript error on `api.tasks.myFunction`** â†’ The `_generated/api.ts` file is stale. Make sure `npx convex dev` is running, or run `npx convex codegen` manually. ### What NOT to do - Do NOT call Convex functions directly from Server Components with `fetch`. Use the Convex HTTP client (`new ConvexHttpClient(url)`) from a Server Component, or use `useQuery` in Client Components. - Do NOT perform side effects (HTTP calls, sending emails) inside queries or mutations â€” only in actions. Side effects in queries/mutations will cause errors and won't be retried correctly. - Do NOT store secrets in Convex function code â€” they will be visible in the Convex dashboard. Use Convex environment variables (`npx convex env set SECRET value`) for secrets accessed in actions. - Do NOT create new `ConvexReactClient` instances inside React components. Create one instance at module level and pass it to the provider. - Do NOT manually edit files in `convex/_generated/` â€” they are overwritten on every `convex dev` / `convex deploy`. - Do NOT skip schema definition for production apps. Without a schema, there is no runtime validation and TypeScript types won't work. - Do NOT use Convex as a replacement for file storage â€” store large files in Convex File Storage or S3/R2 and only store the reference URL in Convex. - Do NOT check `identity.tokenIdentifier` for user ID in queries â€” use `identity.subject` which is the stable user ID.
